feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: Titans urged to sign 21-INT defensive back ahead of Week 1
entry.content: The Titans have improved in several ways this offseason, but they could definitely use some more help in the secondary.

entry.title: Caleb Durbin has gone from Red Sox disappointment to Alex Bregman's long-term replacement
entry.content: Durbin has went from zero to hero for the Red Sox.

entry.title: Pete Hegseth puts cost of Iran war at $37.5 billion as the Pentagon seeks more money from Congress
entry.content: The defense secretary’s estimate, revealed at a combative Senate hearing, is up from the $25 billion Pentagon officials gave in late April. NBC News has reported the amount is much higher.

entry.title: Ex-Warriors NBA champion Jonathan Kuminga gets $45 million Lakers sign-and-trade update
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers continue to be linked to a former Golden State Warriors high upside forward.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Even skyscrapers started out as basements.
Lucky Numbers: 84, 33, 63, 30, 25, 40

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Write your goal in 3 words every morning – helps with distractions.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: A man will ejaculate approximately 18 quarts of semen in his lifetime.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Will Rogers
entry.content: “Nothing makes a man broad-minded like adversity.” — Will Rogers

entry.title: The results of the autopsy of Nolan Wells will be released Wednesday, the family's attorney says
entry.content: An attorney for the family of Nolan Xavier Wells says he will release the results of an independent autopsy into the 18-year-old's death on Wednesday morning.

entry.title: 49ers' 21-game starter reportedly on 'hot seat' entering training camp
entry.content: The 49ers have big expectations for the 2026 season, so one 2025 starter could find himself on the bench if he doesn't improve.

entry.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo reveals approach for recruiting LeBron James to Miami Heat
entry.content: Antetokounmpo doesn't plan to pressure James to join him in Miami.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: One of the biggest majors of the year is here!

entry.title: NFL analyst compares Arch Manning to 49ers QB
entry.content: Arch Manning could be the No. 1 pick in 2027, and one analyst believes he resembles another former No. 1 pick who played in San Francscio.

entry.title: Indiana basketball adds familiar name to roster for FISU America Games
entry.content: A former Indiana basketball player has been added to the roster for the FISU America Games, according to a source

entry.title: Blue Jays’ Jeff Hoffman Sends Phillies Message After Trade Reunion Push
entry.content: The Toronto Blue Jays polarizing reliever offered a Philadelphia Phillies response amid a surprising turnaround on the mound.

entry.title: Jet Greaves seeks $7.5 million. Will the Columbus Blue Jackets blink?
entry.content: The Columbus Blue Jackets submitted a far lower salary than goalie Jet Greaves did for a scheduled July 23 arbitration hearing in Toronto.

entry.title: Sir Alex Ferguson says Kevin Keegan built Newcastle’s ‘best ever team’ as he pays tribute to great rival
entry.content: The former Manchester United boss was famously on the other end of Keegan’s “I’d love it, absolutely love it, if we beat them” rant in 1996

entry.title: Zelensky Removes Ukraine’s Armed-Forces Chief After Days of Street Protests
entry.content: The firing of Gen. Oleksandr Syrskiy comes at a pivotal moment in Ukraine’s four-year-long defense against Russia’s full-scale invasion.

entry.title: Pat McAfee defends his show after being blamed for ESPN firings
entry.content: Someone is to blame for all the layoffs at ESPN

entry.title: Ryan Garcia frustrated with 'disrespectful' Oscar De La Hoya: 'Bro, collect your check and move on'
entry.content: Ryan Garcia and Oscar De La Hoya's long-running public feud has entered yet another chapter.

entry.title: When does Luke Littler play at the 2026 World Matchplay of Darts? Schedule, opponents and more
entry.content: Littler is looking to go back-to-back in Blackpool.

entry.title: Secretary Hegseth grilled over growing cost of war with Iran
entry.content: Iran launched new attacks on U.S. allies in the region today even as Secretary Hegseth made his case to a Senate committee for more funding. The hearing turned contentious with a verbal dispute over leadership. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

entry.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
entry.content: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the diseaseA humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said. Continue reading...

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts Day 4 second round final results, scores, schedule & bracket: Littler hits 9-darter, missing double for another
entry.content: Only 16 players remain heading into Tuesday.

entry.title: 2 married couples from Honduras were among 5 adults who drowned in an Ohio river
entry.content: The skies were mostly clear and weeks of sweltering heat had finally broken as 33-year-old Jose Mario Pineda Dias and his 28-year-old wife, Marina Suyapa Regalado, set out with their two children for some leisurely fishing in an Ohio river. A second Honduran couple and an adult friend joined them.

entry.title: RFK Jr. says cyclosporiasis outbreak is ‘under control’ amid investigation into its origins
entry.content: Mexico said its own investigation hasn’t found evidence linking the outbreak to the Taylor Farms facility in central Mexico.

entry.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
entry.content: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

entry.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
entry.content: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

entry.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
entry.content: Sila's fresh $300 million round will help it make enough of its silicon-carbon anode material to power more than 100,000 EVs.

entry.title: Disney and Kraft Heinz ink multiyear partnership
entry.content: Walt Disney Co. and Kraft Heinz have announced a partnership that would include new items at Disney theme parks and cruise ships, as well as marketing campaigns.

entry.title: Trump administration says it's deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
entry.content: The Trump administration says it's deferring over $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of suspected fraud and noncompliance

entry.title: Rubio Says Nicaragua’s Plans to Cancel Elections Threaten U.S. National Security
entry.content: The Trump administration wants to isolate Nicaragua, as President Daniel Ortega deepens repression to cement his decadeslong grip on power.

entry.title: Sony Music Entertainment files new lawsuit against AI startup Udio
entry.content: Sony Music Entertainment on Monday filed a new lawsuit against AI startup Udio, alleging the company infringed on the copyrights of more than 30,000 recordings.

entry.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
entry.content: The acquisition — Einride's first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business.

entry.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
entry.content: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

entry.title: Ukraine’s Battle-Tested Drone Boats to Be Made in U.S. for the First Time
entry.content: The deal could be the start of a bigger move to share weaponry that Ukraine has honed during its war with Russia.

entry.title: Pixar and National Geographic hit in latest round of Disney layoffs
entry.content: Pixar and National Geographic were affected in the latest round of layoffs at Disney, where several hundred staffers were cut on Tuesday.

entry.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
entry.content: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

entry.title: Tesla spins up robotaxi pilots in Orlando and Tampa ahead of Q2 earnings
entry.content: The company didn't say how many are in each city and has taken a far more cautious approach to scaling the network than CEO Elon Musk had promised.

entry.title: Judge approves a $1.5B Anthropic settlement over pirated books used to train the Claude chatbot
entry.content: A federal judge has approved a $1.5 billion copyright settlement in which artificial intelligence company Anthropic will pay thousands of authors about $3,000 per book after using pirated copies of their works to train its Claude chatbot.

entry.title: Ed Harris says he wanted to quit 'Dutton Ranch,' felt 'underused and inconsequential'
entry.content: Ed Harris opened up on the red carpet about his experience on Taylor Sheridan's 'Yellowstone' spinoff 'Dutton Ranch,' saying he felt 'underused and inconsequential' in the series' first season.

entry.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
entry.content: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

entry.title: The U.S. Puts Once-Unthinkable Ideas on the Table to Disarm Hezbollah
entry.content: Proposals such as enlisting Syria, vetting Lebanese troops or relying on Israel alone face steep hurdles.

entry.title: Yoko Ono's 'Cut Piece' has lost little of its cutting urgency
entry.content: The Broad's Yoko Ono concert at REDCAT in concert with its exhibition reveals just how closely Ono was to John Cage and in the process connects the Broad and Grand Avenue with LACMA.

entry.title: U.S.-China AI talks scheduled for September
entry.content: The U.S. and China are reportedly slated to hold discussions over artificial intelligence in September.

entry.title: Ryan Clark, Karl Ravech exit ESPN in round of layoffs
entry.content: Most of the cuts announced Tuesday are tied to ESPN's acquisition of the NFL Network.

entry.title: His creepy score made 'Obsession' a blockbuster. What's Rock Burwell's next wish?
entry.content: Burwell's score is getting Oscar buzz as 'Obsession' hits streaming. 'I'd be lying if there wasn't a bit of imposter syndrome,' he said.

entry.title: Sexual kinks are no substitute for human connection in Erik Patterson's 'F— Strangers'
entry.content: Echo Theater Company presents the world premiere of provocateur playwright Erik Patterson's "F— Strangers," directed by Chris Fields and starring Michael Sturgis, James Tupper, Sean Luc Rogers and Tasha Ames.

entry.title: Israel Believes Iran Moved Nuclear Centrifuges Into Pickaxe Mountain
entry.content: The U.S. has reviewed the assessment, and President Trump has threatened to attack the site.

entry.title: Facing a debt collection lawsuit? Here's what to do in the next 30 days.
entry.content: A debt collection lawsuit doesn't have to catch you off guard. Here's how to protect yourself in the first 30 days.

entry.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
entry.content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

entry.title: Bets against SpaceX grow to 32% of float as Elon Musk warns short sellers won't survive
entry.content: About 206 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 32% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

entry.title: Canada ready to ‘intensify’ trade talks as Trump reportedly prepares to hit more countries with new tariffs – US politics live
entry.content: Mark Carney says Canada has made proposals to resolve dispute as tariffs are due to be imposed on wide range of goodsSign up for US Breaking News emailUS gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 – almost a dollar more than a year ago.It comes as Brent Crude, the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92 a barrel. Continue reading...

entry.title: Tropical Storm Bertha threatens Gulf Coast with flooding and storm surge
entry.content: Tropical Storm Bertha formed on Monday, threatening the Gulf Coast. Meanwhile in the Midwest, severe storms caused flooding in Des Moines, Iowa, and several tornadoes were spotted in northern Wisconsin. Lana Zak reports.

entry.title: What happens to joint credit cards after one spouse dies?
entry.content: One spouse dies, but what happens to the shared credit card accounts? The answer isn't always straightforward.

entry.title: 2 American tourists stabbed near Acropolis in Athens, Greece
entry.content: Two Greek American tourists were stabbed near the Acropolis in Athens, Greece, police said. CBS News' Anna Coren has more.

entry.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
entry.content: Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites openA Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington.The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator. Continue reading...

entry.title: Light made a flip phone. It’s colorful and it’s cheap.
entry.content: Light co-founder Kaiwei Tang helped create the Motorola Razr, and he's as surprised as anyone that over twenty years later, the flip phone is relevant again.

entry.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
entry.content: TikTok recently settled its portion of the case with the plaintiff ahead of a jury trial that starts next week in Los Angeles. YouTube has also reached a deal, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant.

entry.title: AI music generator Suno breach affects 55M users, per Have I Been Pwned
entry.content: A hacker took names, phone numbers, and physical addresses of millions of customers who used AI music generator Suno.

entry.title: As the U.S.-Iran war heats up again, these parts of the stock market and economy could be affected
entry.content: Investors and economists are deliberating what the latest exchange in hostilities will mean for the broader economy.

entry.title: What to know about the landmark Warner Bros. Discovery sale
entry.content: Learn more about Paramount's planned acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery — a historic Hollywood megadeal valued at $111 billion — as it continues to develop.

entry.title: UK government scraps plans for digital ID cards after millions of Brits opposed
entry.content: The new U.K. government scrapped a digital ID program that millions of British people opposed, in place of a tax cut to tackle the cost of living.

entry.title: Appeals court denies Biden bid to block release of tapes with biographer, for now
entry.content: An appeals court denied former President Joe Biden's attempt to keep the Justice Department from giving a conservative think tank redacted transcripts and audio recordings of conversations with his biographer.

entry.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
entry.content: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

entry.title: Hegseth to testify on Capitol Hill as Democrats press for more Iran war updates
entry.content: Nearly 100 U.S. military service members were injured by Iran's strikes in the last month, CBS News has learned. This comes as the war with Iran falls under increased scrutiny on Capitol Hill. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

entry.title: The Xteink X4 Pro could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
entry.content: Xteink makes its pocket-sized e-reader even better with a touchscreen and front light.

entry.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
entry.content: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

entry.title: What are the best ways to lower your credit card interest rates now? Here's what some experts think.
entry.content: Average credit card rates are sitting near 22% and quietly draining wallets. Here's how to fight back now.

entry.title: 2 soldiers from Texas and Hawaii killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
entry.content: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

entry.title: Oprah gives inside look at her expanding podcast and reveals best guest she's ever had
entry.content: Oprah Winfrey announced she's expanding her podcast, "The Oprah Podcast." Starting on Tuesday, two new episodes will drop weekly. She spoke to "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about her decision and revealed more about the guests she's had appear on the podcast.

entry.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
entry.content: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June

entry.title: Bluecore Energy raises $10M to build portable nuclear reactors on barges
entry.content: Maritime nuclear energy startup Bluecore Energy has raised $10 million in a pre-seed funding round led by Slauson & Co.

entry.title: China AI Companies Rush to Raise Funds and Close Gap With U.S.
entry.content: Researchers and executives at Chinese AI companies say a shortage of investment and chips is holding them back in competition with leading American companies.

entry.title: NBA Power Rankings: How every team's free agency, trades, and draft moves changed the league's landscape
entry.content: A look at NBA power rankings with rosters mostly set for the 2026-27 season.

entry.title: How good will the Bulls be? Predictions for the 2026-27 season
entry.content: TSN's Steph Noh gives his prediction for the Chicago Bulls in the 2026-27 season

entry.title: LeBron James rumors tracker: Latest news on Warriors, Cavaliers and other potential 2026-27 landing spots
entry.content: For likely the last time in his career, all eyes across the NBA are on James' decision. Here's the latest on where the basketball legend will spend his age-42 season.

entry.title: How to watch The Hundred cricket 2026: Live stream, TV channels and schedule for season
entry.content: The Hundred cricket TV channel: Full schedule and live stream information for 2026 tournament.

entry.title: Why Colston Loveland & Bears are treating Packers rivalry like Ohio State vs. Michigan
entry.content: Colston Loveland and the Bears led by Caleb Williams are treating their rivalry with the Packers like it's Ohio State vs. Michigan.

entry.title: Azzi Fudd joins rare WNBA company with defensive feat in Wings’ loss to Liberty
entry.content: Azzi Fudd is known for her shooting, but a historic defensive start has placed the Dallas Wings rookie in the company of a two-time Defensive Player of the Year.

entry.title: Tropical Storm Bertha in the Gulf of Mexico is moving toward the Florida Panhandle and Alabama
entry.content: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico and was moving slowly toward the Florida Panhandle and Alabama, causing a tropical storm warning and storm surge watches, forecasters said.

entry.title: Biggest World Cup risers and fallers: Folarin Balogun, Vozinha and 10 more who helped or hurt transfer stock
entry.content: Which players gave the biggest boosts to their reputations at the 2026 World Cup and who has a rebuilding job to contend with over the coming season?

entry.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
entry.content: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat wellOne in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...

entry.title: 13 bookstores, one weekend and a $325 grand prize: L.A.'s first book crawl is here
entry.content: Participating locations stretch across the entire L.A. region, featuring institutions like Vroman's in Pasadena, the reopened Book Soup in West Hollywood and niche favorites like Culver City's The Ripped Bodice.

entry.title: 8 ways that Christopher Nolan's 'Odyssey' changes Homer's epic
entry.content: A gorier Cyclops, a darker Circe, an entirely new ending: The writer-director felt free to reshape the 2,700-year-old epic poem for the Imax age.

entry.title: Tom Hiddleston calls 'Pompeii: Out of Time' an 'extraordinary confluence of my passions'
entry.content: The actor known for playing Loki in the MCU time travels to Pompeii in his new series for NatGeo, which is part travelogue, documentary and historical reenactment of the explosion of Mt. Vesuvius.

entry.title: Ben Affleck's AI company is sold for more than $500 million
entry.content: Netflix purchased Ben Affleck's AI company in March. A new security filing revealed the streamer giant paid $587 million in cash for the actor's film tech company.

entry.title: Key Arizona primaries to watch today
entry.content: Arizona has traditionally been a red state, and six of its nine House representatives are Republicans.

entry.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens, threatening Gulf Coast
entry.content: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

entry.title: Tourists stabbed near famed Acropolis in Athens, Greece
entry.content: A man stabbed two tourists near the ancient Acropolis in Athens, Greece's top tourist attraction, police said.

entry.title: FCC plans to ban companies selling DJI products under other brands
entry.content: Several companies suspected of selling DJI products under their own brands might soon be banned in the US.

entry.title: FBI agents told to stand down on ICE assault probes; DOJ denies policy shift
entry.content: Homeland Security Investigations is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

entry.title: Supreme Court to hear from Alaskan bush pilot whose plane was confiscated over a six-pack of beer
entry.content: Supreme Court to hear from Alaskan bush pilot whose plane was confiscated over a six-pack of beer

entry.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
entry.content: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...

entry.title: US pledges to crack down on Haitian gangs making money off social media
entry.content: The U.S. envoy to the United Nations says the U.S. government plans to stop violent gangs in Haiti from profiting off social media

entry.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
entry.content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

entry.title: Rand Paul releases docs that reveal FBI halted border inspection of virus researcher over Wuhan lab links
entry.content: Rand Paul releases docs that reveal FBI halted border inspection of virus researcher over Wuhan lab links

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The early bird gets the worm, but the second mouse gets the cheese.
Lucky Numbers: 43, 34, 54, 70, 83, 79

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Keep a “Second Brain” Journal where you keep track of your goals, habits and top 3 daily moments.
